LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SURGICAL CARE AFFILIATES, LLC, GOLDEN GATE ENDOSCOPY CENTER, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AS A DUPLICATE ENTRY. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AT TO GGEC. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AS TO SCA. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AND DENIED IN PART. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE THE CLAIMS OF DISCRIMINATION AND RETALIATION CAN SUPPORT THE CLAIM. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE PUNITIVES CLAIM. THE COURT GRANTS SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON CAUSES OF ACTION 5 AND 6 (DEFAMATION) BECAUSE THE PERSON WHO UTTERED THE DEFAMATION WAS NOT SCA'S EMPLOYEE AND AS STATED, THE STATEMENT WAS PRIVILEGED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS SURGICAL CARE AFFILIATES, LLC AND GOLDEN GATE ENDOSCOPY CENTER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication, the motion for summary judgment is granted at to GGEC. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that plaintiff and GGEC do not have an employment relationship. Therefore, plaintiff cannot establish causes of action 1, 2, 3, 4, and 7. Plaintiff also cannot establish causes of action 5 and 6 because the common interest privilege of CC 47(c) bars the claims. The motion for summary judgment is denied as to SCA. The motion for summary adjudication is granted and denied in part. The motion is denied as to causes of action 1, 2, 4, and 7. While defendant has proffered a legitimate reason for the adverse employment actions (Seidler dec., pars. 20 and 24), plaintiff has presented substantial evidence showing that the reasons were pretextual. At par. 10 of her declaration, plaintiff states that Dr. Aron initiated the conversation regarding Ms. Caramucci and Mr. Vidal also participated in the conversation. Mr. Vidal (who is younger) was not disciplined, however. Ms. Beck declares at paragraph 6 of her declaration that she too complained to Dr. Aron regarding Ms. Caramucci, but she was not disciplined. Ms. Beck is also younger and the trier of fact can infer age discrimination from the disparate treatment. In addition, Kornach stated that she never told Seidler that plaintiff refused to take a break. (Kornack Depo, 125: 18-20). There is also evidence that the reason for the termination was pretextual. At paragraph 11 of her declaration, plaintiff declares that Ms. Kornach never instructed her to start at 8:30 a.m. Mr. Kelly at paragraph 3 of his declaration states that he witnessed the conversation and corroborates plaintiff's version of the facts. The trier of fact could conclude that defendant's proffered reasons for the probation and termination are unworthy of credence. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding the retaliation cause of action. As stated, the trier of fact could find that defendant's proffered reasons for its conduct are pretextual. The adverse employment actions occurred shortly after plaintiff's protected activity. There is evidence of a causal nexus. Mr. Seidler admits that plaintiff was the only person that he witnessed with a copy of the letter. (Seidler's depo, 122:10-24). Plaintiff also mailed the letter to him in an envelope with her own handwriting. (Morch depo., 173:1-2). The court denies summary adjudication on the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action because the claims of discrimination and retaliation can support the claim. FEHA is a cumulative remedy that does not displace the common law. The court denies summary adjudication on the punitives claim. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. In addition, the Shill deposition at page 17 indicates that Mr. Seidler was responsible for 3-4 of SCA's centers and a trier of fact could infer that he was a managing agent that could shape corporate policy. The court grants summary adjudication on causes of action 5 and 6 (defamation) because the person who uttered the defamation was not SCA's employee and as stated, the statement was privileged. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
